hero.title: Argatia
hero.subtitle: Argatia Winery in Rodochori, Naoussa — where tradition meets innovation at 480m elevation.

about.title: Our Story
about.subtitle: Argatia — a tradition born from love for the land and wine
about.body: Our founders united their skills, professional experience, and love for quality viticulture to establish Argatia in 2000. Organic farming, exclusively Greek grape varieties, and wines that express the place they come from.

meta.title: About the Winery | Argatia
meta.description: The story of Argatia Winery. Founded in 2000 by Panagiotis Georgiadis and Dr. Charoula Spintheropoulou in Rodochori, Naoussa.

history.title: Our History
history.founding: Our founders, Panagiotis Georgiadis and Dr. Charoula Spintheropoulou, united their skills, professional experience, their love for quality viticulture and respect for the land, and founded in 2000 the company Georgiadis-Spintheropoulou O.E., under the distinctive title Argatia.
history.vineyards: That same year began the installation of our first vineyards, the cultivation of exclusively Greek grape varieties, and the production of wines that reveal the place they come from. Our organic farming is certified by DHO.
history.tourism: Wishing to contribute to the agritourism development of the region, they created a visitable winery that attracts wine lovers from all over the world.
history.secondGen: In 2025, the founders' children take full control. Christoforos takes over wine production and Konstantinos handles marketing and sales.

argatia.title: What is Argatia
argatia.text: Argatia (or Argateia) is a very important institution of the Pontian agricultural society, which according to S. Efstathiadis (1984) is created and established by the need of circumstances and operates in various ways depending on the objectives pursued each time. The institution of Argatia is considered the supreme manifestation of the collective spirit among the populations of Greek origin in Pontus.

philosophy.title: Our Philosophy
philosophy.organicText: Our vineyards are organically farmed from the very start and certified by DHO.
philosophy.greekText: Exclusively Greek varieties — Xinomauro, Negoska, Malagousia, Assyrtiko, Mavrodafni.
philosophy.terroirText: Wines that reveal their place of origin — expressing the soil, climate, and location's characteristics.

charoula.name: Dr. Charoula Spintheropoulou
charoula.bio: Charoula Spintheropoulou is the viticulturist of Argatia Winery and co-founder of the company. Having dedicated her life to viticulture and the study of Greek grape varieties, she lays the foundation for producing high-quality grapes that showcase indigenous varieties and the region's terroir.

panagiotis.name: Panagiotis Georgiadis
panagiotis.bio: Born and raised in Rodochori, he was active in Naoussa, then Thessaloniki, Athens, and finally returned to Rodochori. His bond and memories from village life defined his business choices. His vision: 'To create in our place.' His goal: the winery he and Charoula Spintheropoulou built to become synonymous with quality Xinomauro and a winery that wine lovers reference.

christoforos.name: Christoforos Georgiadis
christoforos.bio: Graduated from the Oenology School of Drama. He takes over wine production at Argatia with faith in the potential of Greek wines and optimism for the future.

konstantinos.name: Konstantinos Georgiadis
konstantinos.bio: Studied digital marketing at the American Farm School. His goal: the evolution of the winery and the spread of Argatia wines throughout the world.

meta.title: Vineyards & Terroir | Argatia
meta.description: Argatia vineyards at three locations — Krasna, Lakka, Xerolacos. Organic farming, Greek grape varieties.

vineyards.title: Vineyards & Terroir
vineyards.subtitle: Three locations, one vision — wines that reveal their land

organic.title: Organic Farming
organic.text: Our vineyards are farmed organically from the beginning of planting and are certified by DHO. The goal of all applied farming techniques is to achieve plants of moderate vigor and robustness, a necessary condition for producing high-quality wines.

krasna.name: Krasna
krasna.description: Krasna is located at 260m elevation. It is an area with gentle terrain, smooth slopes, and sandy-clay to clay-loam soils. Vineyard installation began in 2000, with Xinomauro (clones V6 and V3), Negoska, Malagousia, and Assyrtiko varieties grafted onto 1103 Paulsen rootstock. In 2019, another Assyrtiko vineyard was planted on the same rootstock.

lakka.name: Lakka
lakka.description: Lakka is located slightly lower at 250m elevation and features lighter sandy to sandy-loam soils with a little calcium carbonate. Vineyard installation began in 2008, exclusively with Xinomauro (clones V6 and V3) grafted onto 1103 Paulsen rootstock.

xerolacos.name: Xerolacos
xerolacos.description: Our newest vineyard is located in Xerolacos, Rodochori, at 550m elevation, with sandy-clay-loam soil and moderate calcium carbonate. It was planted in 2020 with Mavrodafni variety grafted onto 110R rootstock.

meta.title: Our Wines | Argatia
meta.description: Exceptional Greek wines from Argatia Winery. Red, White, and the special Argatia Nevma.

wines.title: Our Wines
wines.subtitle: Exceptional labels from our vineyards in Naoussa

red.name: Argatia Red
red.description: A multi-variety wine with the dominant variety being Xinomauro, paired with Negoska and Mavrodafni. An especially fruity wine with medium body.
red.pairing: Poultry, pasta with red sauce and cheeses

white.name: Argatia White
white.description: Our white wine is produced from Malagousia, Assyrtiko, and Xinomauro varieties. A tank wine that matures on Assyrtiko's fine lees for approximately 2-3 months depending on the vintage.

nevma.name: Argatia Nevma
nevma.description: A unique blend of Negoska and Mavrodafni varieties. This wine was 'born' in 2019 and has since been produced in only 650 bottles, and only when the vintage is exceptional.
